title: New strategy hypothesis selection memo (comparison only)

s26_why_not_paper_ready: entry edge inconclusive (entry not separable from trend filter)
shared 2014-2016 NQ/ES weakness (choppy/non-trending regime collapse, no diversification)
friction-sensitive full-history edge (IS break-even ~0.06R/trade; IS negative at 0.10R)
only partial independent market support (ES is the only independent market vs NQ)

data_reality.implication: Any hypothesis needing intraday bars (e.g. opening-range) or a NEW independent underlying (YM/Dow, RTY/Russell) requires a separately-authorized data fetch BEFORE it can be tested. Hypotheses on daily NQ/ES are testable immediately with full independent multi-market coverage and no new data.

selection_criteria.note: Criteria are chosen to directly attack S26's documented weaknesses, not to re-discover them.
criteria: Separable entry edge -- the entry signal is a discrete, measurable event that can be significance-tested AGAINST the regime filter (fixes S26's inconclusive entry edge).
Counter-cyclical or robust to the 2014-2016 choppy regime that killed S26 (fixes the shared trend-collapse weakness).
Friction headroom -- fewer, larger-R trades rather than many marginal ones (fixes friction sensitivity).
Immediate independent multi-market testability on provisioned daily NQ/ES (avoids partial-support trap and a new data fetch).
Distinct mechanism from Donchian breakout and S26 trend-pullback (avoids re-testing a known-parked family).

candidates.name: Volatility-confirmed trend continuation
candidates.real_entry_edge_why: Adds a volatility-expansion gate (e.g. ATR rising / range expansion) to a trend-continuation entry; thesis is that continuation moves backed by expanding vol persist further.
candidates.avoids_prior_weaknesses: Vol expansion may give bigger moves => some friction headroom vs S26.
candidates.reintroduces_prior_weaknesses: Same trend-continuation family as S26 (close>EMA200 style); the entry is again hard to separate from the trend filter, and it would collapse in the SAME 2014-2016 choppy regime that killed S26.
candidates.friction_sensitivity: moderate (better than S26 if it captures larger expansions, but still many trades)
candidates.regime_risk: HIGH -- same trending-regime dependence and choppy-regime collapse as S26
candidates.biggest_failure_mode: Re-discovers S26's trend dependence: entry significance inconclusive again, 2014-2016 collapse again.
candidates.first_clean_test_design: Daily NQ IS 2013-2022: define vol-expansion gate mechanically, compare continuation entries WITH vs WITHOUT the gate against random-in-regime entries to isolate the gate's marginal edge.
candidates.verdict: WEAK -- too close to the parked S26 family.

candidates.name: Opening range breakout with regime filter
candidates.real_entry_edge_why: Intraday opening-range breakouts can capture early-session momentum; a daily regime filter restricts to favorable days.
candidates.avoids_prior_weaknesses: Different mechanism (intraday momentum) from Donchian/S26; regime filter could sidestep some chop.
candidates.reintroduces_prior_weaknesses: Many small intraday trades with tight stops => HIGH friction sensitivity, exactly the weakness S26 already flagged.
candidates.friction_sensitivity: HIGH -- tight intraday stops make slippage/commission dominate; most likely to FAIL friction stress
candidates.regime_risk: moderate (regime filter helps) but intraday microstructure regime risk is new and unmodeled
candidates.data_requirements: HEAVY -- requires intraday/minute bars NOT provisioned; needs a separate authorized data fetch and a new intraday engine
candidates.biggest_failure_mode: Friction destroys the edge; data + engine cost is large before any signal is even visible.
candidates.first_clean_test_design: N/A without intraday data; would first require a separately-authorized intraday data provisioning step.
candidates.verdict: WEAK NOW -- blocked on data + worst-case friction profile.

candidates.name: Pullback with NQ/ES cross-confirmation
candidates.real_entry_edge_why: Requires both NQ and ES to confirm a pullback setup, on the thesis that cross-market agreement filters noise.
candidates.avoids_prior_weaknesses: Could reduce false signals vs single-market S26.
candidates.reintroduces_prior_weaknesses: CRITICAL methodological flaw: coupling NQ and ES into the ENTRY consumes the only independent validation market -- nothing independent remains to validate against, collapsing multi-market robustness testing. Also 2014-2016 was weak on BOTH simultaneously, so cross-confirmation would NOT have avoided the shared collapse.
candidates.regime_risk: HIGH for the exact failure regime (both markets fell together 2014-2016)
candidates.multi_market_testability: POOR by construction -- it weaponizes the independent market into the signal
candidates.biggest_failure_mode: Destroys independent validation and still shares the 2014-2016 weakness.

candidates.name: Mean reversion after overextension inside a bull trend
candidates.real_entry_edge_why: Long-only: within an established uptrend (e.g. close>EMA200), enter when price is statistically OVER-EXTENDED to the downside (e.g. close well below a short MA / oversold band) expecting a snap-back. The over-extension trigger is a DISCRETE, measurable event distinct from the trend filter -- so its marginal edge can be significance-tested directly (the exact thing S26 could not show).
candidates.avoids_prior_weaknesses: Mechanism is COUNTER-CYCLICAL to S26's failure regime: choppy/range-bound 2014-2016 (which destroyed trend-continuation) is typically FAVORABLE for buy-the-dip mean reversion. Entry is separable from the regime filter, attacking the 'entry edge inconclusive' problem head-on. Fewer, more selective entries give better friction headroom than intraday ideas.
candidates.reintroduces_prior_weaknesses: Still uses a trend gate, so a real bear market is dangerous (falling-knife risk) -- but that is a known, bounded risk addressable by the trend gate and an R-stop.
candidates.friction_sensitivity: moderate -- manageable with defined targets and an R-stop; not the tight-stop intraday trap
candidates.regime_risk: Different and complementary to S26: weakest in sustained downtrends/crashes, STRONGER in the choppy regime where S26 was weakest -- a genuinely different regime profile
candidates.multi_market_testability: EXCELLENT now -- daily NQ + independent ES (plus MNQ/MES corroboration), no new data fetch
candidates.biggest_failure_mode: Catching falling knives in a true sustained downtrend; mitigated by the bull-trend gate and a fixed R-stop -- must be stress-tested in 2018/2020/2022 drawdowns.
candidates.first_clean_test_design: Daily NQ IS 2013-2022, FROZEN before any OOS: trend gate close>EMA200; over-extension trigger defined mechanically (e.g. close <= lower band k*ATR below a short MA, or RSI < oversold threshold within the uptrend); fixed R-stop and R-target; THEN run an entry-significance test comparing the over-extension entries against random entries drawn from the SAME bull-trend bars, to isolate whether the over-extension trigger itself adds edge. Only after IS is frozen, evaluate ES as the independent market and 2023-2025 OOS.
candidates.verdict: STRONGEST -- separable entry edge + counter-cyclical regime profile + immediate independent testability.

candidates.name: Support/resistance breakout-retest with volatility confirmation
candidates.real_entry_edge_why: Enter on the RETEST of a broken S/R level (not the breakout itself), with a vol-confirmation gate; thesis is the retest filters false breakouts and gives a defined-risk entry.
candidates.avoids_prior_weaknesses: Fewer, higher-quality trades => better friction headroom than S26; retest is a more specific, testable entry event.
candidates.reintroduces_prior_weaknesses: Breakout/retest logic fails in chop via false breakouts and failed retests -- the SAME 2014-2016 vulnerability that killed S26; and it is adjacent to the parked Donchian breakout family.
candidates.friction_sensitivity: low-moderate (bigger moves, wider stops -> good headroom)
candidates.regime_risk: HIGH in chop (false breakouts) -- shares S26's worst regime
candidates.biggest_failure_mode: Choppy regime generates false breakouts and failed retests -> 2014-2016 repeat; overlaps parked Donchian family.
candidates.first_clean_test_design: Daily NQ IS 2013-2022: define breakout + retest + vol gate mechanically; isolate the RETEST's marginal edge vs entering on the raw breakout, against random-in-regime entries.
candidates.verdict: MODERATE -- good friction headroom but shares S26's chop weakness and overlaps Donchian.

selected_hypothesis.name: Mean reversion after overextension inside a bull trend
selected_hypothesis.why_selected: It is the only candidate that attacks S26's specific documented weaknesses rather than re-discovering them: (1) its over-extension trigger is a discrete, measurable entry event that CAN be significance-tested against the regime filter -- directly targeting S26's 'entry edge inconclusive'; (2) it is COUNTER-CYCLICAL to the 2014-2016 choppy regime that collapsed S26 and Donchian, giving a genuinely different regime profile rather than the same trend dependence; (3) selective entries with defined R-targets give better friction headroom than the intraday idea; (4) it is immediately testable on provisioned daily NQ + independent ES with NO new data fetch, preserving clean independent multi-market validation (which idea 3 would have destroyed and idea 2 lacks).
selected_hypothesis.primary_risk_to_watch: Falling-knife risk in a sustained downtrend; must be stress-tested through 2018/2020/2022 drawdowns and gated by the bull-trend filter + fixed R-stop.
selected_hypothesis.next_step: S27-D2 -- pre-register the FROZEN S27 mean-reversion strategy spec (exact trend gate, over-extension trigger, stop/target, exit precedence) on daily NQ IS 2013-2022, with the entry-significance test design fixed BEFORE any OOS. No optimization; spec frozen before results.

guardrails.note: This memo selects a direction only. It authorizes no code, no backtest, no data fetch. S27-D2 (spec pre-registration) is itself a separate, to-be-authorized step.

trading_recommendation: NONE. This is a research-direction memo. S26 stays PARKED; Donchian stays PARKED; no new strategy is validated.
